[Human]
Explain list comprehensions in Python.

[Assistant]
List comprehensions provide a concise way to create lists. Example: `[x*2 for x in range(5)]` produces `[0, 2, 4, 6, 8]`.

[Human]
Can I use conditions in them?

[Assistant]
Yes. Example: `[x for x in range(10) if x % 2 == 0]` gives all even numbers up to 9.
